reasoning: A wins on the dimensions that matter most for this prompt: its five concepts spread across the widest set of conceptual axes (collection-source, epistemics/truth, temporality, material impermanence, delivery-timing), and two of them take the boldest swings in the field. The Off-Ramp abandons the museum's defining feature (a building you visit) to intercept people in the 90 seconds when a lesson can still change a decision, and the Compost Heap is a building that physically cannot exist next month because it is fed and depleted by visitors. A also carries the sharpest, most argued risk justification. B is a strong runner-up: distinct theories, a fresh Calibration Casino with a public miscalibration score, and clean craft, but slightly safer overall. D has the best prose and most vivid sensory detail (the Autopsy Theater's instruments and toe tag are excellent), yet it is penalized for convergence: three of its five concepts (self-failing museum, confessional archive, survivorship/near-misses) recur in B and C, so its internal diversity is the weakest. C ranks last because its register leans conventional, the Mortuary and Confessional are familiar moves and the Reversal Hall is a common failure-became-success trope, and its appended frameworks note adds meta-commentary rather than concept value.
